sources.note: June 21 2026: Royal Caribbean's Ovation of the Seas docked in Seward with a dead, pregnant, 61-foot endangered fin whale on its bow

Preliminary necropsy (Alaska SeaLife Center, NOAA Fisheries, Alaska Veterinary Pathology Services): blunt force trauma to jaw, spine, ribs consistent with a vessel strike; official cause of death still pending
Matson-WhaleSpotter partnership announcement; company claim of whale detection up to 3 nautical miles away, day or night; WhaleSpotter built on WHOI-licensed technology
CEO Shawn Henry: system detects whales in time for evasive action more than 90% of the time (company/CEO claim, not independently verified); Matson Alaska routes to Anchorage, Kodiak, Dutch Harbor
WhaleSpotter designed for deployment on container ships, cruise ships, ferries; no cruise-line deployment confirmed in any source
Ovation of the Seas: 4,180 guests at double occupancy, up to 4,905 at max capacity
2026 Annual Deployment Plan for Observers and Electronic Monitoring, Alaska groundfish and halibut fleet. Observer/EM coverage is a federal requirement
